appraisal_events.evidence: « Quel mensonge déprimant était-elle en train de faire à Swann... »; description of her habitual lies and visible distress; the letter reading: « J’ai eu raison d’ouvrir, c’était mon oncle. » leading to the inference that Forcheville had been there and was sent away.
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator reconstructs and confirms Odette’s current deception (with Forcheville present and concealed), framing her as a habitual, fearful liar whose present behavior masks the truth.

status_effects.explanation: Her standing is locally lowered by the narrator’s exposure of a manipulative lie and of her pattern of deceit.
